title: ee.mesh.lane_grant_preview.v1
description: Pre-grant lane visibility audit emitted by the future `ee mesh preview-grant <peer> --lane <lane>` command (SRR6.46.17 / bd-36bbk.1.17). Lets the operator see EXACTLY which memories would become visible to a peer through a proposed lane grant before they run `ee mesh grant`. Read-only invariant: this surface MUST NOT mutate the workspace or emit audit rows; the pure-decision module `src/mesh/lane_grant_preview.rs` enforces this by construction.

peerNodeKey.description: The peer this preview targets. Redactable under the `tailscale_metadata` class.

workspaceId.description: Workspace this preview was computed against. The visibility math is scoped to one workspace at a time.

affectedMemoryCount.description: Total count of memories the lane grant would expose under the proposed policy. Counted across ALL memories, not just the preview sample.

redactedFromExposureCount.description: Count of memories that would still be hidden via redaction-class rules even after the lane is granted. These appear in `affectedMemoryCount` conceptually but `wouldExposeUnderProposedPolicy` is false on their row.

previewSampleStrategy.description: Strategy used to choose which memories appear in `previewSample` when total exceeds `--limit`. `random` is deterministic-random (seeded) so repeated calls with the same seed reproduce the same preview.

previewSample.description: Capped sample of memories that would be exposed. Capped at `LANE_GRANT_PREVIEW_MAX_LIMIT` (500) regardless of caller-requested `--limit`. Ordered per `previewSampleStrategy`.

redactionRulesApplied.description: Names of redaction classes the upstream pipeline applied (e.g. `["api_key", "jwt", "tailscale_metadata"]`). Forwarded unchanged from the caller-supplied input.

cautions.description: UX hazard signals the renderer surfaces visually. The pure-decision module emits up to 7 cautions: `peer_not_in_group`, `lane_already_granted`, `high_trust_class_exposure`, `large_volume_exposure`, `sensitive_tags_in_exposure`, `tombstoned_in_exposure`, `redaction_active`. Severity is `info` or `warning` only; `error` is not a path this read-only computation takes.

nodeKey.description: Stable tailscale node identifier (e.g. `nodekey:<base64>`). Redactable under the `tailscale_metadata` class.

lane.description: The six trust-lane channels a peer-group binding can grant or deny. Matches the field names on `IntendedLanePolicy` in `src/mesh/auto_enrollment_safety.rs`.

trustClass.description: SRR6.5 trust authority class. `human_explicit` is the user's directly-authored material — most sensitive to leak — and triggers the `high_trust_class_exposure` caution when exposed.

contentPreview.description: First 100 characters of memory body (multibyte-safe, char-counted not byte-counted). The caller is responsible for redacting the content BEFORE passing it into the preview; this module only truncates.

hasSensitiveTags.description: True when any tag matches the canonical sensitive vocabulary `{secret, private, personal, internal}` (per `SENSITIVE_TAGS` in the pure module).

redactedFields.description: Names of fields the redaction pipeline already stripped from this memory before it reached the preview module.

wouldExposeUnderProposedPolicy.description: Whether the memory would actually become visible to the peer under the proposed policy. False when the memory is tombstoned, when redaction-class rules still hide it, or when the proposed lane is not `allow`.
